data.title: Significant ASTER Transfer Leads to Price Drop
data.description: A substantial transfer of ASTER tokens has led to a notable price decline. According to ChainCatcher, blockchain analyst Yu Jin reported that an entity, which withdrew 50.1 million ASTER tokens from Aster last September, transferred 34.61 million ASTER tokens back to Aster early today. This movement caused the ASTER price to drop by 5%, from $0.66 to $0.63. The entity's on-chain address still holds 24.25 million ASTER tokens.

data.title: Polymarket's probability of a permanent peace agreement between the US and Iran by April 30th is only 4%, and the market has fallen 7% in the last 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of a permanent peace agreement between the US and Iran by April 30th on Polymarket is only 4%, down 7% in the last 24 hours. The total trading volume of contracts for this event has exceeded $54 million. The contract rules state that if Iran and the US reach a permanent peace agreement by the specified date (11:59 PM Eastern Time), the market will mark it as "yes"; otherwise, it will mark it as "no." A permanent peace agreement is any agreement that explicitly states that military hostilities between the US and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses similar wording to explicitly state that military hostilities between the US and Iran will permanently end. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or do not contain a final agreement that permanently ends military hostilities between the US and Iran (e.g., a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026) do not qualify. The market's primary source of information is official information from the US and Iranian governments; however, other credible reports can also be consulted. US President Trump previously stated that he wouldn't bother going to Pakistan just to get a worthless deal from Iran. Odaily Seer, the prediction channel, continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: The Balancer attackers have exchanged 21,000 ETH for 617.43 BTC in the past three days.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, the Balancer attacker has exchanged 21,000 ETH for 617.43 BTC in the past three days, worth $48.72 million. The attacker currently holds 1,000 ETH, worth $2.32 million, and may sell them off further.

data.title: RookieXBT's linked wallet spent 25,000 USDC to buy 1.89 million SPIKE.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, RookieXBT's associated wallet spent 25,000 USDC to buy 1.89 million SPIKE.

data.title: A whale has accumulated 20.1 million RAVE tokens through Bitget, representing approximately 8.1% of its total circulating supply.
data.description: According to Arkham data, the Bitget exchange's hot wallet address (0x1AB) made a large withdrawal of approximately 20.1M RAVE tokens, worth about $18.458 million, within the past 8 hours. The 20.1M RAVE tokens were distributed to 8 separate addresses, with individual withdrawals ranging from 1.45M to 3.511M tokens. Currently, the circulating supply of RAVE is 248.04M, and this withdrawal represents approximately 8.1% of its total circulating supply. RAVE is currently trading at $0.9203, up 3.14% in the last 24 hours, with a current market capitalization of $228 million.

data.title: An entity transferred 34.61 million ASTER tokens to Aster in the early hours of the morning, worth $22.94 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, the entity that withdrew 50.1 million ASTER from Aster last September transferred 34.61 million ASTER (US$22.94 million) back to Aster early this morning. ASTER subsequently dropped by 5% ($0.66 → US$0.63). The entity's on-chain address currently holds 24.25 million ASTER ($15.82 million).

data.title: Trump: Cryptocurrency is a "huge industry"
data.description: At a cryptocurrency conference, US President Trump stated that cryptocurrency is a "big industry," while also warning bankers not to hinder the progress of cryptocurrency legislation.

data.title: Litecoin disclosed a zero-day vulnerability that triggered a DoS attack and abnormal MWEB transactions; the vulnerability was fixed after a block reorganization.
data.description: Litecoin disclosed in an article on the X platform that a recent zero-day vulnerability led to a DoS attack, affecting the operation of major mining pools. Mining nodes that failed to update in time allowed an invalid MWEB (MimbleWimble Extension Block) transaction to be executed, enabling the related tokens to be withdrawn to a third-party DEX. The Litecoin network rolled back these invalid transactions through a 13-block reorg, confirming that they would not be included in the main chain. During this period, all valid transactions were unaffected. The vulnerability has now been fully patched, and the network has returned to normal operation.

data.title: Kalshi executives: Insider trading is not unique to prediction markets and requires sophisticated solutions.
data.description: John Wang, Head of Crypto at Kalshi, responded to the insider trading controversy surrounding prediction markets on X, stating: “I think this is a very important issue, but it doesn't only exist in prediction markets. The stock market is essentially a prediction market for a company's future performance, and it has been exploring and iterating on the line between ‘legitimate information advantage’ and ‘illegal use of material non-public information’ for a long time. The role of regulation is to find this balance. Like the stock market, insider trading is a complex problem that requires sophisticated solutions, but it is not insurmountable. I also agree that when operating at scale, introducing mechanisms such as KYC and market monitoring is essential to help prevent insider trading. So we have been doing this at Kalshi from day one.”

data.title: Iranian Deputy Speaker: Mojtaba orders the Strait of Hormuz not to return to pre-war status quo.
data.description: Iranian Parliament Deputy Speaker Nikozadeh stated that Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has explicitly ordered that the Strait of Hormuz must not return to its pre-war state. (CCTV News)

data.title: After being dormant for 1.6 years, a wallet spent $7 million to buy 3,017 ETH.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a wallet that had been dormant for 1.6 years spent $7 million to buy 3,017 ETH, at an average price of $2,320. In the past two days, this whale has bought a total of 7,300 ETH, worth $17 million, and may make further purchases.

data.title: Iranian officials say the US and Iran may hold a second round of talks in the coming days.
data.description: An Iranian diplomatic official stated that a second round of talks between Iranian and US delegations is possible in the coming days. It is understood that Iran has conveyed a message to the US side, urging President Trump to reduce threatening rhetoric and indicating that if the US stance softens, hardliners within Iran are more likely to support participation in the negotiations. (CCTV News)

data.title: Aave has established a recovery fund to facilitate the full redemption of rsETH and, in conjunction with KelpDAO and LayerZero, has launched an implementation plan.
data.description: Aave announced that its ecosystem partners and service providers will establish a recovery fund to facilitate the full asset backing of rsETH. This plan takes into account pending Aave DAO governance votes (including Arbitrum governance votes), indicative protocols, and subsequent successful implementation. Aave stated that it has reached an agreement with KelpDAO and LayerZero on the technical steps required to implement the recovery plan, and related work is underway. Addressing the issues of affected users and maintaining the stability of the broader DeFi ecosystem are currently the top priorities. The final recovery plan, user operation procedures, and further updates will be announced soon.

data.title: NBA Playoffs: Timberwolves vs. Nuggets Game 4 Betting Insights
data.description:
A recent monitoring by Odaily reveals that in the Polymarket prediction event for the NBA Playoffs Western Conference first-round Game 4 between the Timberwolves and the Nuggets, an account with a 63% win rate (address: 0x9495425feeb0c250accb89275c97587011b19a27) has invested $310,000 on the Timberwolves to win, with an average opening price of 48¢.

According to Odaily, the NBA Playoffs Western Conference first-round Game 4 between the Timberwolves and the Nuggets is set to begin at 8:30 AM (UTC+8) today. The current series score is 2-1, with the Timberwolves leading. The Nuggets' official injury report has updated Aaron Gordon's status (calf injury) to questionable. In the previous game, Aaron Gordon was absent due to injury, and the Nuggets lost to the Timberwolves 96-113.

data.title: The probability of a US-Iran diplomatic meeting before April 29th on Polymarket has dropped to 16%, a 39% decrease in 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, Polymarket data shows that the predicted probability of a "US-Iran diplomatic meeting before April 29th" has fallen to 16%, a brief drop of 39%. Market rules define a meeting as "yes" if US and Iranian representatives hold a formal diplomatic meeting before 11:59 PM ET on April 29th. A diplomatic meeting is defined as a formal meeting between representatives of both sides in an official capacity and with authorization, including indirect face-to-face meetings through mediators, but excluding brief greetings or chance encounters for non-diplomatic purposes. The meeting must be in-person and recognized by the government or reported by credible media. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing them in.

data.title: TRUMP Falls 14% Despite Donald Trump's Support for Crypto Industry
data.description: TRUMP experienced a 14% decline on Saturday, even as U.S. President Donald Trump expressed his support for the growth of the U.S. cryptocurrency industry. According to NS3.AI, during a speech to major token holders in Florida, Trump endorsed a bill intended to regulate the industry. The 45-minute address also touched on topics such as artificial intelligence, the conflict in Iran, and Trump-branded sneakers, as reported by two attendees and recordings provided to Bloomberg.

data.title: Trump's Crypto Support Fails to Boost Memecoin Value
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump's recent commitment to bolster the U.S. cryptocurrency sector did not translate into increased interest in his associated memecoin. According to Bloomberg, the token, known as $TRUMP, experienced a significant decline, dropping 14% on Saturday. This downturn occurred despite Trump's engagement with some of the largest holders of the token.

The president's efforts to promote the growth of the crypto industry were anticipated to have a positive impact on the value of $TRUMP. However, the market response was contrary to expectations, as the token's value decreased notably. Trump's interaction with key stakeholders in the memecoin did not manage to reverse the downward trend. The situation highlights the challenges faced by niche cryptocurrencies in maintaining investor interest, even when backed by high-profile endorsements. The decline in $TRUMP's value underscores the volatility and unpredictability inherent in the cryptocurrency market, where external factors can significantly influence token performance.

data.title: ECB Collaborates with Standards Bodies for Digital Euro Infrastructure
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) has entered into agreements with three European standards organizations to develop the digital euro using open, non-proprietary payment infrastructure. According to NS3.AI, this framework incorporates CPACE for contactless payments, nexo standards for merchant and ATM connectivity, and Berlin Group rules for account-based transfers. The ECB believes this approach could lessen dependence on Visa and Mastercard. However, the widespread adoption of the digital euro across the euro area will still hinge on the implementation of relevant regulations.

data.title: Litecoin Chain Reorganization Following MWEB Exploit
data.description:
Litecoin experienced a significant chain reorganization after attackers exploited a zero-day vulnerability in its Mimblewimble Extension Blocks (MWEB) feature. According to NS3.AI, the reorganization affected blocks from 3,095,930 to 3,095,943. The Litecoin Foundation revealed that the flaw allowed older mining nodes to accept invalid MWEB transactions, facilitating unauthorized peg-outs and routing funds to third-party decentralized exchanges.

Aurora Labs CEO Alex Shevchenko noted that attackers leveraged this vulnerability to execute double-spending attacks against multiple cross-chain swap protocols, with NEAR Intents exposure estimated at approximately $600,000. The Litecoin Foundation confirmed that the offending transactions were removed and the vulnerability has been fully patched. Despite these measures, some trading venues reported financial losses due to the exploit.
